creator: will@csociety.org
text:
On Fri, Nov 30, 2001 at 02:35:00AM -0800, Eugene M. Kim wrote:
> There are some situations where static KDE libraries will help; one
> example would be to have to run some KDE application where the full
> KDE environment isn't readily available (such as an installer CD-ROM).
> However, although there exist such situations they will usually be
> special applications (i.e. in 90%+ of the normal situations wouldn't
> need static libraries, such as in a desktop environment); this is why
> I conditionalized the build of static libraries.

90%?  I'd say the number is much closer to 100%.

> About the packing list, my patch already takes care of conditional
> inclusion of .a files; are you suggesting that my method was wrong,
> and if so, what would be a good example port for me to refer to?

Ah, you're right, it does do that.  But your method requires an
additional file.. PLIST_SUB does the job fine.

My next problem with supporting such a feature is that it breaks
autogeneration of the PLIST based on an installation.  Or in
other words, if I were to regenerate the PLIST for a new install,
I'd have to remember to add those files manually.  That's not
acceptable.  There must be a better approach.  One such approach
might be to keep a list of static files in the Makefile itself
(which doesn't need to be autogen'd, unlike the PLIST), and
conditionally add them to the PLIST used for the install iff
WANT_STATIC_TOO is defined.

Speaking of "WANT_STATIC_TOO", I think a better variable name
would be KDELIBS_WANT_STATIC, because this operation generally
doesn't apply to other ports (or it'd be WANT_STATIC) and the
"TOO" part seems a bit awkward.

-- 
wca
